name: Robert Kondrk
formerRole: Vice President, iTunes and App Store Content
connectionToCompany: Robert Kondrk was VP of iTunes and App Store Content at Apple, reporting to Eddy Cue, overseeing content strategy and partnerships for Apple's media services
sourceNote: Publicly known Apple executive; reported on in industry press covering iTunes and App Store leadership
verificationNote: Confirmed by Gemini. Currently VP at Amazon Music, formerly VP iTunes and App Store Content at Apple.

name: Roger Rosner
connectionToCompany: Roger Rosner led Apple's productivity applications group and worked within the Services and software organization closely aligned with Eddy Cue's domain
sourceNote: Known Apple VP; noted in Apple organizational reporting structures
verificationNote: Confirmed by Gemini. Roger Rosner is VP Applications at Apple.

name: Jimmy Iovine
formerRole: Advisor and Creative Head, Apple Music
connectionToCompany: Jimmy Iovine joined Apple as part of the Beats acquisition in 2014 and worked directly with Eddy Cue on the launch and development of Apple Music before departing in 2018
sourceNote: Widely reported in press including The Verge and Billboard upon his departure from Apple in August 2018
verificationNote: Confirmed by Gemini. Departed Apple in 2018 and now operates independently.

name: Trent Reznor
connectionToCompany: Trent Reznor joined Apple via the Beats acquisition and served as Chief Creative Officer for Apple Music under the Services umbrella led by Eddy Cue before departing in 2019
sourceNote: Publicly noted Apple Music executive from Beats acquisition; reported in music industry press. Left Apple in 2019.
verificationNote: WRONG COMPANY corrected per Gemini. Reznor left Apple in 2019 and is now an independent musician and composer. companyAffiliation updated to Independent and currentRole updated accordingly.
